key_insight: The paper shows that adding retrieval, error tracing, and counterexample-guided context can improve automated theorem proving for formally verifying blockchain consensus protocols.
engineering_relevance: This could make formal verification of security-critical distributed systems more accessible and efficient by reducing manual proof effort.
method: Upgraded IsabeLLM for the latest Isabelle and Sledgehammer versions
Added Retrieval-Augmented Generation to supply relevant proof context to the LLM
Implemented error tracing and counterexample generation to guide proof repair
evidence_signal: The two IsabeLLM versions are compared on completing the formal verification of Bitcoin’s Proof of Work consensus.
watch_item: Performance gains may depend on the quality of retrieved context and the reliability of generated proof steps in Isabelle.
paper_title: IsabeLLM: Automated Theorem Proving Applied to Formally Verifying Consensus
